concerns.text: Multiple tasks contain unresolved template placeholders ({{claim:c_3ea0f57a}}, {{claim:c_afa0a894}}, {{claim:c_c70ab3de}}, {{claim:c_67f85a02}}, {{claim:c_f874ec02}}). Per FR-001/FR-002, tasks must have concrete verification criteria. Unresolved placeholders prevent constraint verification against spec/plan.

T017 creates docs/reproducibility/dataset_counts.md but SC-001 requires validation scope documentation in docs/reproducibility/validation_scope.md. This file path mismatch violates SC-001's explicit artifact location requirement.

T026 outputs to docs/reproducibility/core_invariants_tabulation.md but SC-015 specifies docs/reproducibility/core_precision_consistency.md as the required documentation location. This violates SC-015's explicit artifact location requirement.

T016 verification references {{claim:c_3a76cda6}} and T040 verification references {{claim:c_dafcb946}}. Per FR-002/FR-013, verification criteria must be concrete and testable. Unresolved claim placeholders prevent verification against spec thresholds.

Revision tasks T073-T086 add functionality beyond original spec (e.g., T074 KnotInfo fallback lookup not in spec). Per Constitution Principle IV, scope creep must be explicitly documented as reviewer-driven additions to preserve traceability.

Revision tasks T073-T086 marked [P] (parallel) but have implicit dependencies (e.g., T076 depends on T073/T075 completion). Per plan.md parallel execution constraints, dependencies must be explicitly documented to preserve execution order constraints.

T065/T066 are documentation-only tasks noting external agents 'do not exist yet' but plan.md Constitution Check marks Principles II/V as PASS without noting this is documentation-only. Per Constitution Principle II/V, compliance status should reflect documentation vs implementation distinction.
